### 50. Richtech Robotics — `RR`  ·  Score 16/100  ·  Unlikely  ·  Builder
*Service robots / Dex humanoid · YTD -33.9% · mcap $709M · screen alpha 86 · closest analog: **None** (rhyme strength 8/100)*

**What it is:** A small-cap service-robotics integrator/reseller positioned as a Robotics-as-a-Service (RaaS) operator. It deploys autonomous beverage/barista robots (ADAM, dual-arm), floor cleaners (Scorpion/DUST-E, allegedly Sparkoz TN10/TN70), delivery/hospitality bots (Matradee, Medbot/Medusa, Titan), and as of CES 2026 a wheeled humanoid (Dex) built on NVIDIA Jetson Thor and trained in Isaac Sim/Lab. Crucially, in the value chain RR is NOT a component supplier or choke-point — it is a DOWNSTREAM SYSTEMS INTEGRATOR and APPLICATION DEPLOYER. Multiple short reports (Capybara Sept 2025; Hunterbrook Jan 2026) allege the hardware is rebranded off-the-shelf Chinese robots (OrionStar, AutoXing, Sparkoz, Keenon) with Richtech stickers. The actual choke-point silicon (Jetson Thor) belongs to NVIDIA; RR is a buyer of the bottleneck, not the owner of it.

**First-principles choke-point test:** From a bill-of-materials and physics standpoint, RR sits at the LEAST defensible layer of the robotics value chain. The genuine choke-points in humanoid/service robotics are: edge-AI compute (NVIDIA Jetson/Thor + CUDA — RR is a customer here), actuators/harmonic-reduction gearboxes and high-torque-density motors (Harmonic Drive, Nidec, Tier-1 suppliers), precision force/torque and tactile sensors, rare-earth magnets, and battery cells — none of which RR makes. A barista/cleaning/delivery bot is an INTEGRATION of commoditized subsystems (mobile base + arm + cameras + an off-the-shelf SoC). There are dozens-to-hundreds of substitutable suppliers (Keenon, Pudu, OrionStar, Bear Robotics, Richtech itself), so there is no non-substitutability and no pricing power — the BotBar NYC and Ethan Capital lawsuits (customers alleging ADAM was 'defective,' couldn't make coffee/boba, swapped for Panbotica) demonstrate that buyers can and do switch in weeks. If the rebranding allegations are true, RR's 'manufacturing' is a markup on Chinese imports — the opposite of a fab with multi-year capacity barriers. There is no fixed-cost fab to drive violent operating leverage; RaaS is a service business with field-tech labor that SCALES costs WITH revenue (management itself notes gross-margin pressure from technical-support/field-service teams). This fails the choke-point test on every structural dimension: not indispensable, not concentrated, not capacity-constrained, no ASP pricing power, no fab operating leverage.

**Alpha / non-consensus findings:**
- TWO SEPARATE SHORT-SELLER FRAUD REPORTS in 4 months: Capybara Research (Sept 30, 2025) titled 'A China Hustle Selling Rebranded Chinese Robots' alleging insider embezzlement, fake partnerships, fabricated Walmart hype; and Hunterbrook (Jan 29, 2026) in which MICROSOFT EXPLICITLY DENIED any commercial partnership — calling it a free, publicly-available AI Co-Innovation Labs week-long sprint that RR re-cast as a 'close collaboration,' triggering a +40% / ~$370M market-cap pop.
- JUNE 2026 FINANCIAL RESTATEMENT (the decisive new datapoint): RR disclosed it will restate FY2024 and FY2025 financials for improper accounting of warrants, errors tied to its Standby Equity Purchase Agreement with YA II PN (Yorkville — a dilution-financing counterparty), and mistakes accounting for Dec-2025 restricted stock awards. Stock fell ~12% premarket. A restatement materially raises going-concern/internal-controls risk and is a hard fragility gate.
- EMBEZZLEMENT TRAIL alleged by Capybara: Dec-2024 bank records show RR wired $400K in four $100K tranches to Satoshi Holdings (Henry Leong), which forwarded matching amounts to Skybound Innovations LLC (a Nevada entity tied to CEO Wayne/Zhenwu Huang); the last transfer froze when Satoshi's account was seized by creditors.
- CHINA-SCAM-STOCK LINKS: large pre-IPO holder Peiyong Han (676,940 shares) is named in an SEC filing as tied to a Chinese syndicate manipulating US-listed China companies; lead independent director Stephen Markscheid linked to multiple delisted/fraud-accused China names. Pre-IPO shares issued at $0.06 to BVI/Singapore shells, IPO at $5 = ~80x in 11 months.
- INSIDER SELLING INTO THE STORY: COO Phil Zheng sold ~100,000 shares at ~$5.11 (~$511K) in late Sept 2025, days before the Capybara report; insiders collectively net sellers (~$1.3M for Zheng over 12 mo). CEO Huang holds ~31% — concentrated insider control with weak minority protections.
- MASSIVE SERIAL DILUTION: shares outstanding ~62.2M at IPO -> ~95.8M (2024) -> ~197.7M (Dec 2025). $38.7M gross private placement (8.5M Class B at $4.55) launched the DAY AFTER the Microsoft announcement. A ~$1B shelf and a Yorkville SEPA ATM provide essentially unlimited dilution capacity.
- DEX HUMANOID = NVIDIA CUSTOMER, NOT CHOKE-POINT: Dex (CES Jan 2026, Automate June 2026 Humanoid Pavilion) runs on NVIDIA Jetson Thor and Isaac Sim — meaning the actual scarce asset (Thor silicon) accrues value to NVDA. Dex is a wheeled platform stitched from RR's Titan base + ADAM arms; no proprietary actuator, sensor, or compute IP disclosed.
- REVENUE IS TINY AND ARGUABLY SHRINKING ORGANICALLY: FY2025 revenue $5.05M (+19% YoY) but Q1 FY2026 revenue ~$1.2M was DOWN ~8.8% YoY (one-time product sales fell), with RaaS only ~$0.3M. The $4M 'China JV deal' (Boyu/Boyu-Tongchuang) is a related-party-flavored cross-border arrangement, not a blue-chip backlog. Price/sales sits near ~150-177x on ~$5M revenue.

**2-yr price-action analog:** IPO Nov 2023 at $5. Immediate speculative blow-off: ATH ~$12.29 intraday / ~$11.10 close on Jan 23-24, 2024 on Ghost Kitchens/MAC/Walmart-adjacent hype. Then a catastrophic ~97% collapse to an all-time low ~$0.30 on Aug 6, 2024 as deals failed to materialize. Through 2025 it became a retail momentum/meme vehicle (52-wk range $0.725-$7.43; one trailing-year print +494%), spiking on each press release. Capybara short report (Sept 30, 2025) hit a high base — ~ -9.5% on Oct 28 amid the fallout and a separate restatement scare. Late-Jan 2026 Microsoft 'collaboration' release spiked the stock ~40%+ (~$370M mcap), enabling the $4.55 private placement; Hunterbrook (Jan 29, 2026) immediately debunked it. June 2026 restatement disclosure dropped it ~12% premarket; now ~$2.30, YTD -33.9%. STRUCTURE VERDICT: This is NOT the NVDA/MU/SNDK template. It is the INVERSE — a Phase-1 'depressed base' only in the superficial sense of being down from highs, but the down-move reflects a BROKEN/UN-BELIEVED thesis (DISQUALIFIER (b)+(c): bleeding to new lows with no real accumulation base, and drawdowns that DON'T recover because the fundamental story is actively being discredited). The chart is a hype-and-fade penny-stock pattern with vertical headline spikes that fail, not a tightening accumulation base before a fundamental breakout.

**Why this analog:** RR does not rhyme with any of the three. NVDA = software-moated compute monopoly; MU/SNDK = 3-5 player fab oligopolies with rigid capacity, rising contract ASPs, and violent fab operating leverage off losses into a supercycle. RR is the structural opposite on every load-bearing beat: it is a downstream integrator of commodity (allegedly rebranded Chinese) robots with dozens of substitutes, ZERO pricing power (customers sue and switch to Panbotica/competitors), NO fab/capacity choke-point, NO fixed-cost operating leverage (RaaS scales labor with revenue), and ~$5M revenue at ~150x+ sales. Where SNDK's small orphaned base was the FEATURE (real choke-point, mispriced), RR's small base reflects a discredited, possibly-fraudulent story (2 short reports + a June-2026 restatement). The only faint rhyme is 'small-cap with a robotics narrative,' which is cosmetic, not structural. Closest is None; if forced, it is an anti-SNDK.

**Game theory:**
- 🟢 **Bull:** The humanoid-robotics wave is real and early; RR is a US-listed, NVIDIA-Jetson-Thor pure-play with a CES/Automate humanoid (Dex), 400+ deployed RaaS units, ~$85M+ cash, brand-name reference logos (Texas Rangers/Globe Life, Hilton, Golden Corral), and a sticky recurring-revenue pivot growing 31% YoY. If short reports are overstated, sentiment rebuilds, RaaS compounds, Dex wins industrial pilots, and the ~$700M cap re-rates as a 'humanoid' name on multiple expansion. A short-squeeze on any credible contract could spike it 2-3x short-term.
- 🟡 **Base:** RR remains a sub-scale (~$5M revenue), deeply loss-making (~ -$16M FY25) momentum/meme stock that lives and dies on press releases. It survives on its cash pile and serial dilution (shelf + Yorkville SEPA), drifting lower over time as dilution and the credibility overhang cap any re-rating. Periodic headline spikes (CES, Automate, a 'deal') that fade. No durable multibagger; a trader's vehicle, not an investment.
- 🔴 **Bear:** The restatement (June 2026) escalates into internal-controls/going-concern findings or a delisting/regulatory action; the embezzlement, fake-partnership, and China-syndicate allegations are substantiated by the SEC; insiders continue selling; dilution accelerates as cash burns; customers keep churning (lawsuits). The stock retraces toward its $0.30-0.72 lows. Equity could be largely impaired. This is the modal tail risk for an alleged 'China hustle' with a fresh restatement.
- 🎲 **Bull-path win probability:** ~9%

**Play-by-play (how it fires):** Sept 30, 2025: Capybara fraud report -> credibility crack. Late-Jan 2026: Microsoft 'collaboration' PR spikes +40% -> $38.7M raise the next day -> Jan 29, 2026 Hunterbrook + Microsoft denial debunk it. June 17, 2026 (today): restatement disclosed (warrants, Yorkville SEPA, RSAs) -> ~ -12%. NEXT MOVES that would be required for a bull multibagger (and why each is unlikely): (1) RR must publish a credible, evidence-backed rebuttal to BOTH short reports AND clean the restatement with an unqualified audit -> low probability given a week-late 10-K and now a restatement; (2) land a VERIFIABLE, named, large commercial contract (not a JV/LOI/'co-innovation lab') -> none verified to date, and the marquee claims (Walmart, Microsoft) were debunked; (3) demonstrate Dex shipping at scale with PROPRIETARY IP and gross-margin expansion -> Dex is a Jetson-Thor integration, no moat; (4) stop diluting -> contradicted by the active shelf/SEPA. Counter-move by the market: any spike is sold into by insiders and used to dilute, capping upside. The sequence that produces 5-50x simply has no structural engine here.

**Key catalysts to watch:**
- Filing of restated FY2024/FY2025 financials and the auditor's conclusion on internal controls (near-dated; high downside skew)
- Any SEC/Nasdaq response to the late 10-K, restatement, and short-report allegations (delisting/halt risk)
- Automate 2026 Dex humanoid demos (June 2026) and any resulting VERIFIED industrial purchase orders (vs. demos/LOIs)
- Next 10-Q: whether organic revenue re-accelerates or keeps declining ex one-time sales; RaaS unit economics
- Further insider Form 4 sales and any new equity draws under the Yorkville SEPA / $1B shelf (dilution cadence)

**Dimension scores (0–100):** criticality 12 · demand inflection 25 · supply pricing power 8 · operating leverage 15 · valuation headroom 20 · price analog 15 · catalyst clarity 18

**Risks:** GOING-CONCERN / INTERNAL-CONTROLS RISK: June 2026 restatement of FY24/FY25 (warrant accounting, Yorkville SEPA, RSAs) plus a week-late 10-K signals material weakness; under the rubric any going-concern/imminent-delisting risk CAPS the score at 54.; FRAUD ALLEGATIONS UNREBUTTED: two short reports (Capybara, Hunterbrook) alleging rebranded Chinese hardware, fake/overstated partnerships (Walmart and Microsoft both effectively debunked/denied), insider embezzlement ($400K Satoshi/Skybound trail), and China-syndicate links (Peiyong Han SEC matter) — RR has not issued a substantive point-by-point rebuttal.; SEVERE DILUTION & UNLIMITED OVERHANG: shares ~62M -> ~198M; active ~$1B shelf and Yorkville SEPA ATM; equity raised opportunistically into hype spikes (post-Microsoft $38.7M placement).; NO CHOKE-POINT / NO PRICING POWER: commodity service-robot integrator with many substitutes; customers have sued (BotBar, Ethan Capital) and switched vendors (to Panbotica), proving zero switching-cost moat.; TINY, NON-ACCELERATING REVENUE AT EXTREME MULTIPLE: ~$5M FY25 revenue, Q1 FY26 revenue DOWN ~8.8% YoY ex one-time sales; ~$700M cap implies ~150x+ sales with ~ -$16M losses.; GOVERNANCE / RELATED-PARTY RISK: dual-class control with CEO ~31%; related-party vendors (IceKredit/Normanton, Sunwing artificial-plants supplier); insiders net sellers; directors with late SEC filings and prior China-delisting associations.; CHINA EXPOSURE / SUPPLY-CHAIN & SANCTION RISK: alleged sourcing from Chinese OEMs and a China JV (Boyu) create tariff, export-control, and reputational risk, plus the China-ADR-adjacent credibility discount.

**Red-team (skeptic's pass):**
- **Verdict:** *story stock / China-hustle — anti-SNDK, likely impairment*
- **Holes in the bull case:** CHOKE-POINT IS INVERTED, NOT SUBSTITUTABLE-AT-THE-MARGIN: RR doesn't own any bottleneck — it is the CUSTOMER of every choke-point in the stack. Dex runs on NVIDIA Jetson Thor + Isaac Sim (verified via CES 2026 PR): the scarce silicon and CUDA software lock-in accrue value to NVDA, not RR. The mobile bases, arms, cleaners and barista units are allegedly rebranded OEM hardware from AutoXing, Sparkoz, Keenon, OrionStar, Reeman, Ufactory, Pudu, SoftBank, Yunji and Wimsha (Capybara Sept 2025). Criticality is genuinely <20, not merely soft.; DEMAND INFLECTION IS A DEBUNKED, PERPETUAL 'NEXT-DEAL' STORY: The two marquee 'inflections' were both falsified. Microsoft told Hunterbrook (Jan 29 2026) the engagement was a 'standard' free customer prototyping program with 'no commercial element'; RR even buried a 'non-commercial technology collaboration' admission in its delayed 10-K while the press release implied a partnership (+44.6% pop). Walmart hype was fabricated per Capybara. There is ZERO verified, named, large commercial purchase order — only CES/Automate demos and LOIs.; PRICING POWER IS NEGATIVE, NOT ZERO: Customers don't just have substitutes — they actively sue and switch. BotBar NYC and Ethan Capital alleged ADAM was defective (couldn't make coffee/boba) and swapped to Panbotica within weeks. A vendor whose customers litigate and replace it has anti-moat, not a moat.; REVENUE IS SHRINKING ORGANICALLY, NOT INFLECTING: FY2025 revenue ~$5.0M with a ~$15.8M net loss (verified); Q1 FY2026 revenue ~$1.2M, DOWN ~8.8% YoY, with RaaS only ~$0.3M. The widely-cited '31% RaaS growth' is growth off a $0.2-0.3M base — a rounding error, not an HBM-style step-change.; VALUATION HEADROOM IS A TRAP, NOT AN ASSET: The analyst scored headroom 20 but a small base is only bullish when the asset is real and mispriced (the SNDK setup). Here the small base reflects a possibly-fraudulent, serially-diluting micro-cap. At ~$2.30-$2.46 and ~$430-470M cap on ~$5M revenue, P/S is still ~90x; even netting ~$85M cash, EV/S is extreme. There is no 'mispriced choke-point' to re-rate.; OPERATING LEVERAGE RUNS THE WRONG WAY: RaaS is a field-service/labor business — management itself flags gross-margin pressure from technical-support/field-service headcount. Costs scale WITH revenue. There is no fixed-cost fab, so there is no violent incremental-margin flywheel; this is the structural opposite of MU/SNDK.; PRICE-ACTION ANALOG IS FORCED / INVERSE: The chart is hype-spike-and-fade (IPO $5 -> ~$11-12 Jan-2024 -> ~$0.30 Aug-2024, ~-97%; meme spikes in 2025; +40% Microsoft pop immediately debunked; -12% on June-2026 restatement). This is template DISQUALIFIERS (b) bleeding to new lows with no accumulation base and (c) drawdowns that don't recover because the thesis is actively discredited — the inverse of a Phase-1 base.; NO REBUTTAL EXISTS: Across three short reports (Capybara, Hunterbrook, Fugazi), RR has never issued a substantive point-by-point rebuttal; its only 'response' was to point Hunterbrook at a delayed 10-K, then go silent when pressed. A bull re-rating requires a credible defense that has not materialized in 8+ months.
- **Overhype flags:** 'Microsoft collaboration' (Jan 27 2026, +44.6%) — explicitly denied by Microsoft as a free, no-commercial-element customer program; now the basis of multiple securities class actions (Hagens Berman, Robbins LLP) with an Apr 3 2026 lead-plaintiff deadline.; 'Walmart partnership' hype — alleged fabricated per Capybara Research (Sept 30 2025).; '$328.5M cash runway' figure floating in analyst summaries is an artifact/error — actual liquidity is ~$33M cash + ~$52.6M short-term investments (~$85M) as of June 30 2025.; Bull '400+ deployed RaaS units / reference logos (Texas Rangers, Hilton, Golden Corral)' framing implies enterprise traction, yet RaaS revenue is only ~$0.3M/quarter and total revenue is declining — the logos are pilots/placements, not a backlog.; 'Dex humanoid' positioned as proprietary humanoid IP — it is a Titan base + ADAM arms stitched onto an NVIDIA Jetson Thor; no proprietary actuator, sensor, or compute IP disclosed; zero verified purchase orders.; Analyst internal inconsistency: cites both ~$700M cap (P/S ~150-177x) and the current ~$430-470M cap (~$2.30); the lower, verified figure is correct — ~90x P/S — still extreme but the $700M anchor overstates the multiple.; '$1B shelf + Yorkville SEPA = unlimited dilution capacity' is real and confirmed by the ~62M -> ~224M share-count blowout (CNBC/Webull May 2026, even higher than the ~198M Dec-2025 figure cited).
- **Strengths that survived scrutiny:** The company is not (yet) an imminent zero: it held ~$85M in cash + short-term investments as of mid-2025 and management characterizes the restatement adjustments as primarily NON-CASH, so the modal near-term path is slow dilutive bleed rather than instant insolvency. This delays — but does not remove — going-concern risk and is the only genuinely surviving element of the bull case.; The humanoid/service-robotics TAM and demand wave are real and early — but this accrues to the actual choke-point owners (NVIDIA, actuator/gearbox Tier-1s), not to a downstream integrator; RR has optionality to ride sentiment-driven squeezes (a credible 2-3x short-term spike on any headline remains possible), which is a trader's feature, not an investment thesis.; RR is a genuine US-listed Nasdaq name with real (if commodity) product deployments and brand reference logos, giving it more liquidity and headline-spike potential than a pure pre-revenue shell.
- **Sharpened call:** RR is the structural inverse of the picks-and-shovels template: it is a downstream integrator that BUYS every choke-point (NVIDIA Thor/CUDA silicon, allegedly-rebranded Chinese OEM hardware) and owns none, so the value of the robotics wave routes around it. The game tree is dominated by the issuer's own actions: management's repeatable move is to manufacture a headline (Microsoft, Walmart, Dex, a China JV), ride the meme spike, and immediately monetize it via the $1B shelf / Yorkville SEPA ATM — confirmed by the $38.7M placement launched the day after the debunked Microsoft pop and a share count that has ballooned ~62M -> ~224M. Every spike is therefore sold into by insiders and dilution, capping upside; meanwhile the credibility overhang compounds (three short reports unrebutted, three securities class actions, embezzlement and China-syndicate allegations, the June-2026 multi-year restatement of warrants/Yorkville/RSAs with prior financials declared unreliable and a fresh auditor at CBIZ). The decisive gate — going-concern/internal-controls risk from the restatement plus a late 10-K — caps the score at 54, and layering maximum fragility (no choke-point, negative pricing power with customers suing/switching to Panbotica, ~$5M and declining revenue, labor-scaling RaaS with no operating leverage, ~90x P/S, ~31% insider control, China sourcing) drives it to the floor. There is no sequence of dated catalysts that produces a 5-50x re-rating; the realistic distribution is a continued dilutive bleed toward the $0.30-0.72 lows with a fat left tail of delisting/regulatory impairment and only a thin, transient squeeze right tail that insiders will dilute into.

---

## Appendix — Scoring Rubric

REFINED "POTENTIAL SCORE" 1-100 = probability-weighted magnitude of a NVDA/MU/SNDK-style multibagger re-rating over 1-3 yrs. Score the JOINT likelihood the stock (a) rhymes with the price-action archetype AND (b) delivers the fundamental re-rating. Score each dimension 0-100, then SYNTHESIZE (gate, do not average).

DIMENSION SCORING (anchor each to NVDA/MU/SNDK):
1. CRITICALITY / INDISPENSABILITY (choke-point + moat). 90-100 = software-locked monopoly (NVDA/CUDA) or sole-source non-substitutable part. 70-89 = oligopoly choke-point, 3-5 suppliers, multi-year switch cost (MU/SNDK). 40-69 = differentiated but substitutable. <40 = commodity with many vendors. TEST: name every firm that can supply this and the buyer's switching time.
2. DEMAND INFLECTION (dated step-change off a depressed base). 90-100 = a specific, near-dated catalyst with order/booking evidence and prior-cycle capex cuts (HBM sold-out, NAND capacity sold-out 2026/27). 70-89 = clear inflection, evidence building. 40-69 = credible story, no dated proof. <40 = narrative only. TEST: cite the catalyst date and a backlog/booking number.
3. SUPPLY CONSTRAINT & PRICING POWER. 90-100 = rigid capacity (years to add) + rising ASPs/contract prices (+50-100% QoQ) + 'sold out through next year.' 70-89 = tight supply, firm pricing. 40-69 = balanced. <40 = oversupply / price-taker. TEST: is ASP rising and is new capacity >4 quarters out?
4. OPERATING LEVERAGE (off a low earnings base). 90-100 = loss/near-zero -> supercycle, >5x forward-EPS expansion modeled (MU, SNDK ~13x). 70-89 = strong margin expansion, 2-5x EPS. 40-69 = linear margins. <40 = margin compression. TEST: model incremental gross margin and forward-EPS multiple.
5. VALUATION HEADROOM (small base = more upside). 90-100 = small/mid cap, tiny vs TAM, low coverage, post-washout (SNDK orphan ~$28). 70-89 = mid cap with multi-x room. 40-69 = large cap, partial re-rate done. <40 = already mega-cap / priced-in. TEST: market cap vs serviceable TAM; how much of the move is already gone.
6. PRICE-ACTION ANALOG (Phase 1 base or Phase 2 trigger). 90-100 = tightening multi-month base OR fresh breakout on catalyst, dips bought. 70-89 = early uptrend, structure intact. 40-69 = extended but trending. <40 = either already 30-50x (late) or making new lows with no base. TEST: which of the 4 phases is it in?
7. CATALYST CLARITY & GAME-THEORY WIN PROBABILITY. 90-100 = clear near-dated path (signed contracts, design wins, capacity online, supportive policy) where THIS firm structurally wins. 70-89 = strong path, some contingency. 40-69 = plausible but contested. <40 = no clear win path. TEST: name the next 2-4 dated catalysts and why this firm beats rivals.

FRAGILITY DISCOUNT (subtract from the synthesized score; this is a gate, not a footnote): -5 to -30 for dilution risk / equity overhang; -5 to -40 for cash burn or going-concern; -10 to -30 for single-customer concentration (>40% one buyer); -10 to -30 for commodity competition with no discipline; -10 to -50 for China-ADR / delisting / sanction risk; -5 to -20 for thin liquidity / un-borrowable / no institutional ownership. ANY going-concern or imminent-delisting risk CAPS the score at 54 regardless of story.

SYNTHESIS RULE (gate, do NOT average): Dimensions 1 (criticality), 3 (pricing power), and 4 (operating leverage) are LOAD-BEARING — if any of the three is <50, cap the total at 69 (criticality+pricing+leverage are what separate a real choke-point from a story). Dimension 6 (price-action) and 7 (catalyst) confirm TIMING — if both are <50 the setup may be real but early/late, cap at 74. A true elite (85-100) needs >=80 on dimensions 1-4 AND a Phase-1/Phase-2 chart AND low fragility. Weight the LOW dimensions more than the high ones (a chain is as strong as its weakest choke-link).

BANDS: 85-100 = elite multibagger setup (rhymes with all six DNA beats, low fragility); 70-84 = strong asymmetric candidate (one load-bearing dimension soft or timing early); 55-69 = credible but flawed (missing pricing power or operating leverage, or extended chart); 40-54 = speculative/lottery (story without dated demand/pricing proof, or material fragility); <40 = unlikely to parallel (commodity, oversupplied, or going-concern). Be concrete: every dimension score must cite a number or date.
